summary:
The options detailing hepatic conversion into 25-OH-D and renal activation via α-hydroxylase seem accurate based on standard physiology texts referenced here. However, associating symptoms characteristic specifically of Williams Syndrome—such as cognitive impairments or early pubertal development—with broader aspects of vitamin D regulation lacks supporting scientific consensus within our current scope. Therefore,

** \(\boxed{\text{D}}\) stands out due its misalignment with known associations pertinent solely to genetic syndromes versus systemic endocrine functions regulated independently across populations needing adequate supplementation under certain conditions such as lack of sunshine exposure alone does not suffice fully explaining all listed attributes attributed wrongly herein towards one condition over others more broadly applicable ones affecting wider demographics similarly requiring monitoring/adjustment accordingly during deficiency scenarios especially among vulnerable groups
